quickId: Forewing two-toned: pale base, coppery outer two-thirds
Resting moths held tent-like over body
Larvae produce silk webbing in food
Often first noticed flying around lights
habitatNotes: Larvae contaminate grains, dried fruit, pet food, and birdseed with frass and webbing.
management: Find and discard the source — every package. Pheromone traps confirm eradication, not control.

quickId: Six tooth-like projections on each side of pronotum
habitatNotes: Cannot attack whole sound kernels; thrives on broken grain, flour, cereal, dried fruit.
management: Discard infested product, vacuum shelves, use sealed containers.
